title: Split installation docs into guide and reference

sources.reason: Primary installation guide to shorten into a focused adoption lifecycle.
Repository entry point that should expose a concise Quick Install path.
Stack and validation reference that remains the source of truth for preset details.
Workflow and status conventions that the reorganized docs should continue to reference.
Canonical terminology for guide and reference labels.

riskAssessment.reason: The change only reorganizes documentation and links, with no runtime behavior change.

executionDecision.reason: Scope, sources, acceptance, and verification are explicit for a doc-only split.

preReviewWarnings: Check that the README quick path stays concise while preserving required metadata markers and install-command invariants.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: README.md exposes a concise Quick Install entry that points readers to the full installation lifecycle doc.
docs/installation.md is shorter and keeps only the core adoption lifecycle, with upgrade, distribution, and troubleshooting content moved to separate reference pages.
A new documentation architecture draft explains the guide/reference split and the role of each documentation page.
guidelines: Keep README and installation content version-neutral where possible and avoid embedding release-specific narrative in the guide path.
Preserve existing documentation metadata markers and the published make-target references required by the repository checks.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
